response.justification: While Aisha demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and broad technical experience, she is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The position mandates native-level Japanese proficiency (absent from resume), deep C expertise for embedded firmware development (only a brief 2014-2015 mention, with career focused on web/backend), and specialized knowledge in embedded security, networking protocols, and Linux kernel internals (none evident). Her background in web services, mobile apps, and high-level languages does not transfer effectively to low-level embedded security firmware development.
strengths: 20 years of professional software development with consistent emphasis on code quality, testing, and craftsmanship
Proven ability to learn diverse technical stacks across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, open-source projects, and community leadership
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed (native-level required); resume shows only English, Romanian, and basic German
Minimal and outdated C/C++ experience (single role in 2014-2015); career heavily weighted toward JavaScript/TypeScript and web-backend technologies
No demonstrated expertise in embedded security, networking protocols (Layer-2/3, MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel analysis, or embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C programming depth and embedded systems experience
No security domain or networking specialization relevant to embedded firmware
